AdvisorSecurity.description: Security level for the advisor agent: controls what actions it may take autonomously.

oneOf.description:
Advisor can only answer questions and present diffs — never starts a goal or
applies a draft autonomously.
Advisor presents the exact `ta run "..."` command for the human to copy-paste.
At ≥80% intent confidence, advisor fires `ta run` directly without prompting.

TeamRole.description:
Role of the agent/team member producing or consuming an action.

Data-defined (per `TA-CONSTITUTION.md` §1.6): this is an open role name, not a
closed enum, so a `team.toml` can declare any role (e.g. `"security-team"`)
without a TA core change. The five well-known names below are recognized as
constants/constructors for defaults and documentation but are not exhaustive.

persona.description: Resolved persona name, if any tier bound one.

priority.description: Resolved priority relative to other pending requests.

rationale.description:
One line per resolution step, most-specific-tier-first, e.g.
`"agent: tier=persona value=claude-opus-4-8"`. Always populated,
always surfaced to a human (Observable & Actionable).

resolved_workflow_template.description:
Workflow template `ta-workflow::intent::resolve_intent` matched
against the request text at or above its own confidence threshold,
when no `workflow_name_or_path` was given explicitly (v0.17.0.12.23).
Folds workflow-template matching into `route()` as one signal among
several, rather than a second, parallel intent system a caller would
need to consult separately.

security_tier.description:
Resolved security tier — reuses the existing `AdvisorSecurity`
tri-state (read_only/suggest/auto) rather than a new trust-level
concept, per `docs/design/ta-concepts-and-architecture.md` §3.

team.description: Which team role handles this request (§3's "Role" node).

workload_confidence.description:
Confidence in `workload_type` when it was inferred (1.0 when
explicit). Gates the `security_tier = "auto"` tier — see `route()`.

workload_type.description:
The workload type this request was classified as (e.g. "bugfix",
"docs", "security") — either explicit or inferred.
